steps.title: Spot the defining detail in the anomaly
steps.content:
The passage specifies that papers discussing sounds from tractor engines appeared in the “Twitter” search results.

This is the key signal: the word “twitter” itself means to make chirping or twittering sounds — like those machines produce.

The overlap between the search term's meaning and these papers' subject matter is the crucial bridge.

steps.title: Match the word's dual meaning to the explanation
steps.content:
C captures this logic exactly: “twitter” can mean both the social media platform and the sounds machines make.

Papers discussing tractor engine sounds would contain the word “twitter” in the sound-related sense and thus be indexed by the database.

The other three choices don't explain how these sound-related papers ended up in the “Twitter” search results.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
